What is the App Store?

The CypherView App Store lets you build and publish web apps that embed directly inside the CypherView platform. Your app is a standard web page hosted on your own server — CypherView loads it in an iframe and passes contextual data to it.

Platform Support

Not all integration points are available on every platform:

Integration PointWebFlutter (Mobile)
tripDetails✓✓
positionActivityStats✓✓
appSidebar✓—

Before you start, you'll need:

  • A hosted HTTPS endpoint — the URL CypherView will load in an iframe
  • An icon image accessible via HTTPS (optional)
  • A health check URL that returns 200 (optional but recommended)
The app creation wizard is at /app-store/editor or via Create App on your Creator Dashboard.

Basic Info

This step defines your app's identity in the marketplace.

FieldRequiredNotes
NameYesDisplay name shown in listings
Short DescriptionYesOne-liner (max 100 characters)
DescriptionYesFull description on the app detail page
Icon URLNoHTTPS URL to your app icon; defaults to a placeholder
Health Check URLNoHTTPS endpoint pinged to verify your app is reachable
Health Check URL — CypherView sends a HEAD request to this URL before loading your app on mobile. If it fails or times out (5s), users see an "unavailable" tile with a retry button instead of your app.

Visibility

Controls who can discover and install your app.

OptionDescription
globalListed publicly — anyone can find and install it
ourOperationsVisible only within your own organisation
unlistedHidden from marketplace; installable via invite only

Fee Model

OptionDescription
FreeNo charge to installers
Per InstallationFixed ZAR amount charged once when an org installs
Per Active DeviceZAR amount × number of active devices in the org
A fee amount is required if you select a paid model. The wizard shows a live cost preview as you type.

Integration Points

Integration points define where your app embeds inside CypherView. You must select at least one.

Screen IDDisplay NameWebFlutterDescription
tripDetailsTrip Details Screen✓✓Embedded inside a specific trip's detail view
positionActivityStatsVehicle Activity Screen✓✓Embedded in the vehicle activity stats panel
appSidebarApp Sidebar✓—Persistent sidebar in the main navigation (web only)

For each screen you select, you must provide a Base URL — the HTTPS URL CypherView loads in an iframe for that screen.

Context data passed to your app

CypherView delivers context via postMessage after your iframe signals READY:

ScreenData passed
tripDetailstripId, assetId, startDate, endDate, vehicleName, accessToken
positionActivityStatsassetId, startDate, endDate, accessToken
appSidebaraccessToken

Permissions

Declare what CypherView data your app needs. These are shown to users at install time. Format: resource:action

Fleet

ResourceAvailable Actions
assetlist, get, create, update
devicelist, get, create, update
driverlist, get, create, update
triplist, get
positionlist, get

Configuration

ResourceAvailable Actions
assettypelist, get
assetcategorylist, get
devicetypelist, get
geofencelist, get, create, update

Reporting

ResourceAvailable Actions
reportlist, get, run
schedulelist, get, create, update, schedule
alertlist, get, create, update
Selecting certain resources auto-grants read access on related ones — e.g. selecting trip also implies asset, driver, and position.

Review & Submit

The final step shows a summary of all your inputs. You have two options:

Save as Draft

Saves your app without submitting for review. Your app is created but invisible in the marketplace. Use this to continue editing later.

Submit for Review

Submits your app to the CypherView team. A dialog appears asking for:

  • Changelog — describe what's in this version (required)
  • Version Bump (for updates after first approval):
    • patch — bug fixes, no new features
    • minor — new features, backward compatible
    • major — breaking changes

After Submission

Review takes 24–48 hours. Status flow:

draft → pendingReview → approved
                       → changesRequested → pendingReview (resubmit)
                       → rejected
Once approved, you receive a clientId and clientSecret — shown once after approval. Store them immediately. These are your OAuth credentials for calling the CypherView API from your backend.

How It Works

Your app is a standard web page hosted on your own server. CypherView loads it inside an iframe and delivers contextual data (trip ID, asset ID, access token, etc.) via the browser's postMessage API.

You do not need any CypherView SDK — just a web page that speaks the postMessage protocol.

The Handshake

  1. CypherView loads your baseUrl in an iframe
  2. Your app posts a READY message to the parent window
  3. CypherView responds with a WIDGET_CONFIG message containing context data
  4. Your app renders using that data
CypherView may re-send WIDGET_CONFIG without reloading your iframe when the user navigates to a different trip or asset. Handle this — don't assume config is only received once.

postMessage Protocol

Step 1 — Signal ready

Once your page has loaded, post a READY message:

window.parent.postMessage({ type: 'READY' }, '*');

Step 2 — Receive config

Listen for WIDGET_CONFIG from CypherView:

window.addEventListener('message', (event) => {
  if (event.data?.type !== 'WIDGET_CONFIG') return;

  const {
    tripId,       // string | undefined
    assetId,      // string | undefined
    startDate,    // string | undefined  (ISO 8601)
    endDate,      // string | undefined  (ISO 8601)
    vehicleName,  // string | undefined
    accessToken,  // string | undefined
  } = event.data.payload;

  renderApp(event.data.payload);
});

Minimal example

window.addEventListener('message', (event) => {
  if (event.data?.type !== 'WIDGET_CONFIG') return;
  const { tripId, accessToken } = event.data.payload;
  document.getElementById('content').textContent = `Trip: ${tripId}`;
});

// Signal ready after registering the listener
window.parent.postMessage({ type: 'READY' }, '*');

Context Data per Screen

All fields are optional — guard against undefined.

FieldTypeAvailable on
tripIdstringtripDetails
assetIdstringtripDetails, positionActivityStats
startDatestring (ISO 8601)tripDetails, positionActivityStats
endDatestring (ISO 8601)tripDetails, positionActivityStats
vehicleNamestringtripDetails
accessTokenstringall screens

Context Updates

CypherView may re-send WIDGET_CONFIG without reloading your iframe when the user navigates between trips or assets. Make sure your listener updates the UI on each call.

Using the Access Token

The accessToken is a Bearer token scoped to the permissions your app declared at registration. Use it to call the CypherView API:

const res = await fetch(
  'https://api.cypherview.io/telemetrystore/trips/' + tripId,
  { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${accessToken}` } }
);
The token is tied to the installing organisation's session. Only resources covered by your declared permissions will be accessible — other requests return 403 Forbidden.

clientId & clientSecret

After your app is approved, you receive a clientId and clientSecret. These are OAuth credentials for authenticating your backend server when calling the CypherView API on behalf of an installer — separate from the per-session accessToken.

Security

Validate event.origin in production to prevent other sites from sending fake config:

const ALLOWED_ORIGIN = 'https://app.cypherview.io';

window.addEventListener('message', (event) => {
  if (event.origin !== ALLOWED_ORIGIN) return;
  // safe to process
});
  • Your baseUrl must use HTTPS — HTTP is rejected
  • Treat the accessToken as a short-lived credential — don't persist it

Health Check

If you provide a healthCheckUrl, CypherView sends a HEAD request before loading your iframe (5-second timeout). Return 2xx to confirm your service is up. On failure, users see a retry tile.

iframe Size Constraints

ScreenHeight on FlutterWidth
tripDetails267px (fixed)100%
positionActivityStats130px (fixed)100%
appSidebarFull height (web only)Fixed sidebar width
On web, heights are not fixed. On mobile, the heights above are enforced — design compact UIs for those screens.
